svg.text: Substring Search — Naive vs. KMP / Z / Rabin-Karp
For each i in 0..n-m, compare text[i..i+m] to pattern
Always correct; pathological on text="aaaaab" pattern="aaab"
KMP — preprocess pattern's longest proper prefix-suffix array
On mismatch, jump using LPS instead of restarting; O(n + m)
Used as the lib (strstr) and in the Aho-Corasick generalisation
Z-algorithm computes a similar array directly on text+"#"+pattern
Rabin-Karp — rolling hash O(n + m) expected
Compute hash of pattern; slide a window over text computing hash incrementally
On hash match, verify literal substring equality (collision check)
Polynomial hash mod a large prime: h = (h * base + c) mod p
Roll: h = (h - text[i-m] * base^(m-1)) * base + text[i]
Best for: multiple patterns simultaneously, plagiarism detection, fingerprinting
For interviews: KMP is the classic; Rabin-Karp is the elegant.
